body:
**Scope.** Split out of #195. The Tier-1 **Jepsen** consistency leg from #146 (M3.8) / proposal 0005 §13.2 (`0005:408`) was not built. #195 ships the device-mapper (`dm-flakey`/`dm-error`) disk-fault harness; this issue tracks the separate Jepsen leg: a harness asserting consistency over the custodian repair/reconstruction path under partitions and crashes. What exists today is **inert dispatch scaffolding** — `xtask/src/faults.rs::run_jepsen` gates on `WYRD_TIER1=1` and shells out to an externally-supplied `WYRD_TIER1_JEPSEN_CMD` that does not exist in-repo; only the opt-in gating decision is unit-tested, there is no real harness.

**Definition of done**
- [ ] off-Check **harness code** exists and is itself exercised (unit tests over the harness's host-independent logic), not inert dispatch scaffolding gated behind `WYRD_TIER1` / `WYRD_TIER1_JEPSEN_CMD`
- [ ] a **privileged CI job** runs the Jepsen suite green; kept out of the unprivileged `cargo xtask ci` (ADR-0016)
- [ ] the harness asserts consistency over the repair path under partitions/crashes — no stale or torn reads, and repair is neither lost nor duplicated — and a bug-finding run is promoted back as a permanent regression (ADR-0009)

**Refs:** split out of #195 (Tier-1 disk-fault harness); follow-on to #146 (M3.8, only Tier-0 landed); ADR-0016 (unprivileged `xtask ci`); proposal 0005 §13.2 (`0005:408`), §reconstruction.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: M3.11 — Tier-1 Jepsen consistency harness over the repair path — harness + privileged CI job
